title: Test
bullets: Introduce Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) as a technique to improve LLM responses.
Explain the core components of a RAG pipeline: Retrieval and Generation.
Detail how a RAG system uses a vector database to find relevant context.
Describe the process of feeding the retrieved context into a Large Language Model (LLM).
Highlight the iterative nature of RAG, where the LLM generates a response based on the context.
Discuss the benefits of RAG, such as reduced hallucinations and increased accuracy.
Show how RAG enables LLMs to access and utilize external knowledge sources.
